summary: The simulation failed due to a single mismatch at the first clock edge, likely caused by the uninitialized output register Q in TopModule. The reference module may implicitly initialize its output to 0, while the DUT leaves Q uninitialized, resulting in an X or undefined value at the first sample. To resolve this, explicitly initialize Q to 0 in the RTL declaration. This should align the initial state of the DUT with the reference and eliminate the first-cycle mismatch.
risks: If the reference module uses a different initial value for Q, this fix may not fully resolve the mismatch.
Explicit initialization may not be synthesizable on all FPGA/ASIC flows, but is generally supported for simulation and most FPGA tools.
next_steps: Re-run simulation to confirm that the first-cycle mismatch is resolved and all benchmark tests pass.
If mismatches persist, compare the initial value of Q in the reference module and adjust accordingly.
